overview: Verification of proposed changes to D-Lib Magazine snapshots revealed discrepancies and inaccuracies. After re-examining the snapshots, the following changes were confirmed or corrected. The archive cannot confirm the exact dates of some changes due to missing or truncated snapshot data. The verification process focused on verbatim presence of quotes and relevance to the user's focus on substantive changes.

changes.afterText: D-Lib Magazine suspended publication of new issues in July 2017.
changes.confidenceReason: The exact quote appears verbatim in the snapshot dated September 25, 2017, and it is clearly an editorial change. Confidence 75/100 — quote match 100%, agreement 75% across samples.
changes.verificationNotes: Q1, Q2, Q3 passed; Q4 passed; Q5 passed
changes.archivalReason: Content present in the later capture but absent earlier.

changes.description: Contact email changed from [email] to [email protected] in February 2024.
changes.confidenceReason: beforeText and afterText are verbatim-verified, but the change is minor and could be an artifact. Confidence 75/100 — quote match 100%, agreement 75% across samples.
changes.verificationNotes: Q1 passed; Q2 passed; Q3 corrected.
changes.archivalReason: Both versions verified in their captures — a genuine wording/policy change.

replayWarnings: 1 script resource(s) not preserved in this capture
2 script resource(s) not preserved in this capture
captureGaps: gaps over 60 days between snapshots 1 and 2, and between snapshots 6 and 7

a11yAudit.verdict: Accessibility appears to have IMPROVED over time.

F E A T U R E D D I G I T A L C O L L E C T I O N The Kent State Shootings Digital Archive contains featured digitized content from the May 4 Collection, an archive established by the Kent State University Libraries in 1970, containing over 300 cubic feet of primary sources related to the Kent State shootings and their aftermath. [ National Guard personnel move toward Taylor Hall after tear gas has been deployed, Kent State, May 4, 1970 . This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License . ] | Mirror Sites E D I T O R I A L S T A F F Laurence Lannom, Editor-in-Chief Allison Powell, Associate Editor Catherine Rey, Managing Editor Bonita Wilson, Contributing Editor C O N T R I B U T O R S D-Lib thanks Hesburgh Libraries, University of Notre Dame , for their long time support of the magazine, and recognizes the contributions of the following organizations that also support the continued open access publication of D-Lib Magazine . D-Lib Magazine is produced by Corporation for National Research Initiatives . Prior to April 2006, the magazine was sponsored by the Defense Advanced Research Project Agency (DARPA) on behalf of the Digital Libraries Initiative under Grant No. N66001-98-1-8908, and by the National Science Foundation (NSF) under Grant No. IIS-[phone] From 2007 through 2016, contributions by subscribers to the D-Lib Alliance provided financial support for the continued open access publication of D-Lib Magazine. snapshots.fullText: D-Lib Magazine Search D-Lib: HOME | ABOUT D-LIB | CURRENT ISSUE | ARCHIVE | INDEXES | CALENDAR | AUTHOR GUIDELINES | SUBSCRIBE | CONTACT D-LIB D - L I B M A G A Z I N E ISSN: [phone]| https://doi.org/10.1045/dlib.magazine D-Lib Magazine suspended publication of new issues in July 2017. Corporation for National Research Initiatives will continue to maintain the D-Lib Magazine archive , however, suggestions for long-term archiving are welcome, as are thoughts from the community on any alternate usage of the D-Lib brand that would benefit the research community that has been served by D-Lib's virtual pages over the last two decades. Send suggestions to [email] . D-Lib Magazine was produced by Corporation for National Research Initiatives . Prior to April 2006, the magazine was sponsored by the Defense Advanced Research Project Agency (DARPA) on behalf of the Digital Libraries Initiative under Grant No. N66001-98-1-8908, and by the National Science Foundation (NSF) under Grant No. IIS-[phone] From 2007 through 2016, contributions by subscribers to the D-Lib Alliance provided financial support for the continued open access publication of D-Lib Magazine. In particular, D-Lib thanks Crossref , and Hesburgh Libraries at University of Notre Dame for their long-time membership in the D-Lib Alliance. Privacy Policy Copyright © 2017 Corporation for National Research Initiatives D-Lib is registered in the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office.

Corporation for National Research Initiatives will continue to maintain the D-Lib Magazine archive , however, suggestions for long-term archiving are welcome, as are thoughts from the community on any alternate usage of the D-Lib brand that would benefit the research community that has been served by D-Lib's virtual pages over the last two decades. Send suggestions to [email protected] . D-Lib Magazine was produced by Corporation for National Research Initiatives . Prior to April 2006, the magazine was sponsored by the Defense Advanced Research Project Agency (DARPA) on behalf of the Digital Libraries Initiative under Grant No. N66001-98-1-8908, and by the National Science Foundation (NSF) under Grant No. IIS-[phone] From 2007 through 2016, contributions by subscribers to the D-Lib Alliance provided financial support for the continued open access publication of D-Lib Magazine. In particular, D-Lib thanks Crossref , and Hesburgh Libraries at University of Notre Dame for their long-time membership in the D-Lib Alliance.
